video:video.video:title: Author Ann Patchett drives the literary conversation
video:video.video:description: From her bookstore in Nashville, Tennessee, author Ann Patchett is showcasing other literary greats, while continuing her work as a prize-winning novelist with several bestsellers.

video:video.video:title: US bombs Iranian military sites and Kuwait is hit by drone and missile fire, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Air sirens went off across Kuwait City earlier on Monday as Tehran struck Kuwait in response to the United States bombing military sites in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Six people injured in Russian strike on Odesa
video:video.video:description: Six people were injured in an overnight Russian drone attack in Odesa, Maryna Averina, from the State Emergency Service of Odesa region reported on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election which is widely expected to be won by the ruling party
video:video.video:description: Polls opened Monday in Ethiopia in an election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia bets on AI personal computers with new chip powering Windows laptops
video:video.video:description: Nvidia unveiled new powerful chips on Monday that would bring advanced artificial intelligence functions into laptops and desktop computers.

video:video.video:title: Making 'The Lost Boys' fly
video:video.video:description: For their third Broadway show, husband-and-wife choreographing team Lauren Yalango-Grant and Christopher Cree Grant faced a high stakes challenge: They were asked to make vampires fly for the stage adaptation of cult '80s movie, "The Lost Boys."

video:video.video:title: Nvidia bets on AI personal computers with new chip powering Windows laptops
video:video.video:description: Nvidia unveiled on Monday new powerful chips that would bring advanced artificial intelligence functions into laptops and desktop computers, with the new personal computer models from brands including Microsoft and Dell set to roll out later this year.

video:video.video:title: The Greatest - 10 years since death of boxing icon Muhammad Ali
video:video.video:description: Muhammad Ali was a giant of his time - a furious and loud fighter whose influence was felt far beyond the ring.

video:video.video:title: Rescue diver in Laos on the difficulties of searching for and rescuing trapped villagers
video:video.video:description: Rescue workers in Laos said Saturday they had safely evacuated four villagers trapped in a flooded cave for 10 days, the day after another one was successfully extracted.

video:video.video:title: Bob Mackie reflects on sketching famous gown worn by Marilyn Monroe
video:video.video:description: Marilyn Monroe, who died on August 4, 1962 at the the age of 36, would have turned 100 years old June 1. (May 31)

video:video.video:title: Rare blue micro moon seen from Chile
video:video.video:description: People gathered in Chile on Saturday night to view a rare blue micromoon, with visitors taking part in guided observations and astronomy activities at an observatory in San José de Maipo.

video:video.video:title: Moscow aims to set world record with mass table tennis tournament
video:video.video:description: The world record for the highest number of participants in a table tennis festival is expected to be set in Moscow on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military footage said to be of troops capturing strategic castle in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military released footage said to be of troops capturing a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon in the deepest incursion into the country in more than a quarter-century.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrikes hit southern Lebanon as troops capture Beaufort Castle near Nabatiyeh
video:video.video:description: Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency reported that Israeli warplanes carried out multiple airstrikes across villages in southern Lebanon on Sunday, causing casualties, though it did not provide a breakdown of the victims.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Albania against work on luxury development linked to Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner
video:video.video:description: Protesters in Albania clashed with private security guards at a site where a luxury development project linked to Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner - the daughter and son-in-law of US President Donald Trump - is planned.

video:video.video:title: Macron congratulates Paris Saint-Germain after their second Champions League title victory
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron congratulated the Paris Saint-Germain soccer on Sunday after their second Champions League title victory.

video:video.video:title: Newark mayor imposes curfew around Delaney Hall after clashes over immigration detention center
video:video.video:description: The mayor of Newark has imposed a curfew around an immigration detention center in New Jersey after a series of intense clashes between protesters and police Saturday night.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 31
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, May 31: Israeli army says it captured strategic castle in south Lebanon; WHO reports five patients have recovered from rare type of Ebola virus; Colombians are voting in first round of presidential election; Double boom from meteor heard in multiple states near Massachu

video:video.video:title: Fans welcome Argentina team to Kansas City with cheers and songs ahead of World Cup matches
video:video.video:description: Cheering fans greeted reigning FIFA World Cup champions Argentina as the team arrived in Kansas City to begin preparing for its title defense.

video:video.video:title: Death Cab for Cutie's Ben Gibbard: 'Fans are always right'
video:video.video:description: Death Cab for Cutie frontman Ben Gibbard explains why he paid so much attention to the band's fans when writing the indie group's 11th studio album, "I Built You a Tower."

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election, with the ruling party expected to win
video:video.video:description: Polls opened Monday in Ethiopia in an election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party.

video:video.video:title: Extended bar hours seek to boost businesses during World Cup
video:video.video:description: Pennsylvania is one of many states signing off on extending bar and restaurant hours during the world’s most-watched sporting event. (AP Video: Tassanee Vejpongsa)

video:video.video:title: Thousands visit University of Michigan arboretum for yearly peony peek
video:video.video:description: Visitors are making the annual visit to the University of Michigan to see and smell one of the world’s premier collections of herbaceous peonies.

video:video.video:title: Jack White: Musician turned artist
video:video.video:description: Musician Jack White explains why now is the perfect time for him to launch his debut art show in London.

video:video.video:title: Strike hits a group of people in the area of Gaza City's port, according to Shifa hospital
video:video.video:description: Two people were killed and at least 10 injured when a strike hit an area at Gaza City port Sunday, according to Shifa hospital that received casualties.

video:video.video:title: Israeli forces make historic push inside Lebanon and complicate an Iran deal
video:video.video:description: Israeli forces have made their deepest incursion inside Lebanon despite a nominal U.S.-brokered ceasefire and the first direct Israel-Lebanon talks in decades.

video:video.video:title: Blast at building storing explosives in Myanmar kills more than 45 people
video:video.video:description: A blast on Sunday at a building in northeastern Myanmar said to have been storing explosives for mining has killed more than 45 people, according to rescue workers and independent media reports.

video:video.video:title: US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean in fourth attack this week
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it carried out another strike Saturday on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing three men in the fourth attack this week.

video:video.video:title: Jerome Powell strongly defends Fed independence while accepting JFK Profile in Courage Award
video:video.video:description: Former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell used one of his first major public appearances since stepping down as chairman to strongly defend the independence of the central bank, saying that could destroy "priceless" credibility.

video:video.video:title: Ike Barinholtz teases Madonna cameo in season two of ‘The Studio’
video:video.video:description: Speaking at the Peabody Awards, Ike Barinholtz discussed Madonna’s cameo in the upcoming second season of “The Studio," adding, "They wanted to go bigger and better in season two."

video:video.video:title: Southern Japan braces for Typhoon Jangmi
video:video.video:description: Japan’s southern region of Okinawa was preparing on Monday for the approaching Typhoon Jangmi, which is expected to bring heavy rain and strong winds.

video:video.video:title: Fans paint London streets red with flares to celebrate Arsenal's Premier League victory
video:video.video:description: Thousands of jubilant fans gathered along London's streets on Sunday to celebrate successful seasons for both the Arsenal men's and women's teams.

video:video.video:title: A century since birth of Hollywood icon Marilyn Monroe
video:video.video:description: It's been a century since the birth of Hollywood icon Marilyn Monroe on June 1, 1926.

video:video.video:title: Flamingos signal hope amid restoration efforts in Venice's fragile lagoon
video:video.video:description: Beyond Venice’s crowded canals is a fragile lagoon ecosystem. The scientists and environmental groups working to restore these wetlands say a sharp rise in the number of flamingos may be a sign their efforts are paying off.

video:video.video:title: Residents flee Beirut's southern suburbs after Israel orders strikes
video:video.video:description: Traffic jams were seen in Southern Beirut, Monday, as residents attempted to flee after Israel ordered strikes on Dahiyeh where Hezbollah enjoys widespread support.

video:video.video:title: Signs of revival of interest in faith among young people in Madrid, as pope visits
video:video.video:description: Traveling to Spain in June and France in September, Pope Leo XIV will find traditionally Catholic but now staunchly secular countries with an abundance of historic churches but few people in the pews.

video:video.video:title: World Cup dream brings hope to Haiti amid violence and instability
video:video.video:description: Haiti’s qualification for the 2026 World Cup is offering many people across the country a rare sense of hope as the Caribbean nation struggles through one of the most difficult periods in its recent history.

video:video.video:title: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani skips annual parade celebrating Israel but pledges big police presence
video:video.video:description: Crowds of people lined the streets in New York on Sunday to attend an annual parade honoring Israel.

video:video.video:title: Philippines' President Marcos and Japan's PM Takaichi sing 'Imagine' and 'Hey Jude' during state dinner
video:video.video:description: Philippines President Ferdinand Marcos Jr and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi were seen singing along to hits, including 'Hey Jude' by The Beatles and 'Imagine' by John Lennon during a state dinner in Tokyo on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 1
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 1st: Kuwait says it activated air defenses due to missile and drone attack; Trump expresses optimism over U.S.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds protest outside Kenya's Ebola quarantine center for US citizens
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of youths in Kenya’s central town of Nanyuki on Monday demonstrated against the establishment at the Laikipia Air Base of an Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus.

video:video.video:title: Matt Groening celebrates the longevity of "The Simpsons"
video:video.video:description: Matt Groening and James L. Brooks celebrate the longevity of "The Simpsons," as producer Brooks is celebrated at the Peabody Awards.

video:video.video:title: French navy intercepts Russia-linked oil tanker in the Atlantic
video:video.video:description: The French Navy, with support from the United Kingdom, has intercepted an oil tanker under international sanctions that was traveling from Russia, the latest effort by nations that support Ukraine to target Russian oil exports helping to finance President Vladimir Putin’s war.

video:video.video:title: Pro-Trump candidate pulls ahead in Colombia presidential vote as ruling party sows doubt in results
video:video.video:description: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race in the first round of voting Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of Colombia’s outgoing President Gustavo Petro who questioned the results of the election.

video:video.video:title: Widespread Israeli strikes continue in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Widespread Israeli strikes hit towns in southern Lebanon as its ground forces continued to push deeper into Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Atlantic hurricane season forecast to be milder than normal thanks to El Nino
video:video.video:description: Meteorologists predict a developing El Nino could dampen the upcoming Atlantic hurricane season, but it won't eliminate storms.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief reports 5 Ebola recoveries as a new treatment center opens in eastern Congo
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization has reported five patients have recovered from a rare type of Ebola virus in eastern Congo.

video:video.video:title: Duet with Madonna for Barinholtz?
video:video.video:description: Ike Barinholtz discusses Madonna’s cameo in the upcoming second season of “The Studio."

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Kenya call for national crisis to be declared over gender-based violence
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of protesters took to the streets of Kenya’s capital on Monday, calling for the government to urgently investigate rising cases of gender-based violence and femicide, or the killing of women.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 1
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, June 1st: Protesters demonstrate against Ebola quarantine center for Americans in Kenya; Demonstrations across Israel against mandatory enlistment in Israel's military; Florida sues OpenAI, claiming the company and its CEO concealed risks to the public; Peonies bloom an

video:video.video:title: Climate change is fueling more extreme weather
video:video.video:description: From stronger hurricanes to longer droughts and heavier rainfall, a warming planet is intensifying weather extremes and making them more frequent.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Albania's capital over luxury resort project linked to Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people took to the streets of Tirana on Monday to protest against a luxury resort project linked to U.S. President Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner.

video:video.video:title: ICE detainee's daughter describes medical neglect in immigration detention centers
video:video.video:description: An investigation by The AP and KFF Health News found immigrant detainees in 33 states alleged the U.S. is failing to provide them with adequate medical care.

video:video.video:title: FIFA opens international broadcast center ahead of 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: FIFA officially opened its International Broadcast Center in Dallas on Monday, unveiling the facility that will serve as the broadcast and technology hub for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

video:video.video:title: DHS secretary praises state cooperation at New Jersey ICE facility
video:video.video:description: Markwayne Mullin says if needed, he has a plan to pull CBP officers to help with security at the Delaney Hall facility, but says the state is working to provide security there so it’s not needed right now.

video:video.video:title: Death Cab for Cutie's new album is about grief — and it's for the fans
video:video.video:description: "The fans are always right." Ben Gibbard focused on fan response when crafting songs for Death Cab for Cutie's 11th studio album, “I Built You a Tower.”

video:video.video:title: Boots Riley on the bigger message behind 'I Love Boosters'
video:video.video:description: Boots Riley wants "I Love Boosters" to spark discussions around "how the working class can have power."

video:video.video:title: Peruvian shamans perform ritual of sound and smoke to bless candidates ahead of presidential runoff
video:video.video:description: Peruvian shamans gathered on Monday to perform a ritual for the two contenders vying for the country's presidency.

video:video.video:title: What happens when Tim Allen and Tom Hanks go out to eat?
video:video.video:description: "Toy Story" stars Tim Allen and Tom Hanks share funny interactions they've had with fans while dining together.

video:video.video:title: Muhammad Ali's widow reflects on his legacy of compassion 10 years after his death
video:video.video:description: The Ali Center in Muhammad Ali's hometown of Louisville, Kentucky, is sponsoring a “Day of Compassion” this week to promote acts of service and caring.

video:video.video:title: Malaysia enforces social media ban for children under 16
video:video.video:description: Malaysia on Monday began enforcing rules barring millions of children younger than 16 from having social media accounts, joining a global effort to tighten online safety protections for young users.

video:video.video:title: Colombians react after Pro-Trump candidate takes spotlight in Colombia's presidential race
video:video.video:description: Colombians woke up on Monday to the news that the bombastic pro-Trump lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella pulled ahead in the country's presidential race in the first round of elections over the weekend.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Five killed in explosion and fire at defense company in Daejeon, South Korea
video:video.video:description: An explosion and fire occurred at a defense company in South Korea on Monday, killing five people and injuring two others, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Asif Ali and Saagar Shaikh on return of 'Deli Boys' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Asif Ali and Saagar Shaikh are back for more "Deli Boys" as the comedy begins streaming its second season on Hulu and Disney+.

video:video.video:title: ‘Andor’ creator Tony Gilroy urges ambition for future of ‘Star Wars’ franchise
video:video.video:description: Tony Gilroy, the creator and director behind the acclaimed "Star Wars" spinoff "Andor," accepted a Peabody Award for the series’ second season and reflected on what he hopes to see from the franchise moving forward: “I like to think that they’ll stay ambitious.”

video:video.video:title: Indian minister says engagement with Myanmar will continue, as country's leader visits New Delhi
video:video.video:description: India said it will continue engaging with Myanmar after Prime Minister Narendra Modi held talks Monday with the leader of the country’s military-backed government, despite Western sanctions imposed after the military seized power in 2021 (AP video shot by Rishi Lekhi).

video:video.video:title: 8 crested ibises released in Japan decades after extinction
video:video.video:description: Eight crested ibises were released into the wild in a north-central Japanese town, decades after the birds went extinct in the country.

video:video.video:title: Constance Zimmer is glad 'Love Story' brought back '90s nostalgia
video:video.video:description: Constance Zimmer, who stars as Carolyn Bessette's mother in the TV series 'Love Story,' says she's glad 'that side of the story was represented.' (June 1)

video:video.video:title: Residents flee Beirut's southern suburbs after Israel orders strikes
video:video.video:description: Traffic jams were seen in Southern Beirut, Monday, as residents attempted to flee after Israel ordered strikes on Dahiyeh where Hezbollah enjoys widespread support.

video:video.video:title: Only on AP: Under Notre Dame cathedral, a 'dig of the century' unearths 1,700 years of history
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists are digging beneath Notre Dame Cathedral to explore as far back as Roman Paris from 2,000 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Tensions linger between Republicans and White House over the 'anti-weaponization' fund
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration said Monday it will comply with a court ruling temporarily blocking a nearly $1.8 billion fund meant to compensate allies of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Ike Barinholtz on playing Elon Musk: 'After spending time in that world, I want out'
video:video.video:description: Ike Barinholtz, who portrays Elon Musk in director Luca Guadagnino's upcoming film "Artificial," said that immersing himself in Musk's world left him eager to move on: "After spending time in that world, I want out."

video:video.video:title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
video:video.video:description: The politically powerful ultra-Orthodox parties have won exemptions for their followers to forgo military service and instead study in religious seminaries.

video:video.video:title: Michelle Pfeiffer says she learned 'OnlyFans' can be 'all kinds of things'
video:video.video:description: Michelle Pfeiffer, who stars in 'Margo's Got Money Troubles,' says the show helped her learn more about the online platform.

video:video.video:title: Serena Williams is returning to pro tennis at age 44 after nearly four years away
video:video.video:description: Serena Williams is returning to professional tennis at age 44, accepting a wild card entry into a doubles tournament nearly four years after her last competitive match and fueling speculation about a possible Wimbledon comeback.

video:video.video:title: Why Leo's first encyclical went viral and how it speaks to his papal approach
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has issued a manifesto calling for robust regulation of artificial intelligence. His first encyclical, “Magnifica Humanitas,” has sparked widespread online reactions, with many young people praising his willingness to address AI's rapid rise.

video:video.video:title: Giving birth on a dirt road: conflict makes childbirth risky in parts of Africa
video:video.video:description: Women are 40 times more likely to die in pregnancy or childbirth in Central African Republic than in the United States, according to the United Nations.

video:video.video:title: Aid cuts deepen maternal health crisis for Sudan refugees in Central African Republic
video:video.video:description: In remote communities near the Sudan-Central African Republic border, pregnant women who fled Sudan’s war are struggling to access health care as clinics close and aid funding dries up.

video:video.video:title: The Chinese empress dress trend soaring in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Studios near the Forbidden City in Beijing are transforming visitors into empresses from ancient China as young people explore a revival of traditional culture.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian cities of Dnipro and Kharkiv hit in overnight Russian drone and missile strikes
video:video.video:description: Russia attacked Ukraine with a barrage of missiles and drones overnight, killing at least 11 people, injuring dozens and trapping others, authorities said on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Police investigate Iowa man suspected of shooting 6 of his relatives and then himself
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Iowa are investigating the fatal shootings of six people who they believe were killed by a relative who took his own life when confronted by police Monday.

video:video.video:title: Mental fitness exam ordered for man charged with murder in three 'heinous' killings in Hawaii
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors say the killings of three men on Hawaii's Big Island were “especially heinous, atrocious, or cruel.”
